title: Aureline device-registry and settings-sync seed row
description: Boundary schema for one settings_sync_state_row_record published by the M1 device-registry and settings-sync seed. Each row binds one device participation profile to (1) a device participation_state_class drawn from the canonical active/paused/revoked/forgotten lifecycle, (2) a sync session_state_class drawn from the closed sync-session vocabulary, (3) a typed conflict_review_class drawn from the conflict-review vocabulary later UI surfaces consume verbatim, (4) the offered_resolution_path_class set the row exposes (so surfaces cannot invent their own keep-local / keep-synced / merge / rollback / decline paths), (5) the resolution_state_class lifecycle stage the row sits in, (6) the non_widening_posture_class declared on the row's outgoing scope bundle, (7) the scope_broadening_verdict_class the row exposes (so widening cannot ride the same packet shape as a normal apply), (8) the local_ownership_marker_class that separates local-authoritative, managed-authoritative, and policy-authoritative state, (9) a data_class_portability_set covering portable / machine_local / excluded / policy_owned classes the row exposes, and (10) a named failure drill the validation lane reproduces. This schema is a registry entry contract, not a runtime sync engine: it does not implement transport, conflict detection, or merging.

settings_sync_state_row_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for settings-sync state rows. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

sync_state_profile_id.description: Stable, machine-readable id for the sync-state profile row (for example active.local_authoritative.no_conflict). Lowercase dot/underscore/hyphen segments only so the id is safe to log, safe in support exports, and safe in release artifacts.

device_participation_state_class.description: Frozen device participation-state vocabulary. 'active' is the normal participating state. 'paused' is a user- or admin-initiated freeze that retains the device's lineage cursor but emits no bundles and consumes no arrivals. 'revoked' is a durable refusal that rejects all sync traffic. 'forgotten' is the terminal state after the revoke retention window; only the device_id and revocation stamps remain so the audit stream stays complete.

device_class.description: Coarse device-class vocabulary used by the seed. Raw hostnames, MAC addresses, serials, and user ids never appear; only the class travels.

os_family_class.description: Coarse os-family class used by the seed. Raw os versions, kernel builds, and hostnames never appear; only the class travels.

identity_mode_class.description: Identity mode at the moment of seed registration. A device whose identity_mode changes mints a new device_id under migration; the old profile carries through the revocation lifecycle rather than being rewritten in place.

sync_session_state_class.description: Frozen sync-session state vocabulary. 'open' is a live, non-degraded session. 'local_authoritative_degraded' is the floor whenever transport is unavailable, payload stale, capability missing, or policy blocks; local values remain authoritative. 'refused' covers durable refusals (revoked device, encryption failure, policy block, schema incompatibility). 'paused' covers user- or admin-initiated session pause. 'manual_continuity_in_progress' covers the typed export/import path users carry by hand.

conflict_review_class.description: Canonical conflict-review vocabulary. Later UI surfaces (settings UI, support exporter, mutation-journal renderer, migration center, conflict review surface) MUST present one of these tokens; surfaces MUST NOT invent their own conflict status names. 'no_conflict' and 'value_equal_no_op' are the only no-write outcomes safe to suppress from the UI.

conflict_resolution_state_class.description: Resolution-state lifecycle stage the row sits in. 'pending' is the initial state. 'previewed' is reached after a preview render. 'acknowledged' is reached after the user reviews and signs off. 'resolved' is terminal-applied. 'declined' is terminal-refused. 'expired' fires when an acknowledgement times out. 'withdrawn' fires when the producer device is paused/revoked/forgotten before apply. 'not_applicable' applies to no-conflict / value_equal_no_op rows that never enter the lifecycle.

offered_resolution_path_class.description: Resolution path the row offers. 'keep_local' is always available so the user always has a safe out. 'keep_synced' is offered only when the entry did not widen trust and does not violate allowed-scope rules. 'merge_preview' is offered only when the delta is merge-safe. 'rollback_friendly_review' is the default path whenever the setting requires a rollback checkpoint or an approval ticket. 'decline' is terminal refusal.

non_widening_posture_class.description: Posture the row's outgoing scope bundle declares for the non-widening invariant. 'non_widening_affirmed' means the producer set non_widening_affirmed = true. 'non_widening_not_required' applies to rows that do not emit bundles (paused/revoked/forgotten or manual-continuity-in-progress with no bundle on the wire). 'non_widening_affirmation_missing' represents the invariant-violation state used by the failure drill; the validation lane forbids it on protected rows.

scope_broadening_verdict_class.description: Scope-broadening verdict the row stamps on its entries. 'would_not_widen_trust' is the normal case. 'would_widen_trust_refused' captures the refusal path (paired with conflict_review_class = scope_broadening_refusal). 'narrowing_only' captures rows that only narrow local values. 'not_applicable' applies to rows that do not exchange entries.

local_ownership_marker_class.description: Local-vs-managed ownership marker. 'local_authoritative' means the device retains final say over its local values regardless of session state. 'managed_authoritative' means a managed sync service (M1: not yet built) would own the row when it lands. 'policy_authoritative' means admin policy owns the row and sync cannot override it. 'machine_local_excluded' marks rows whose data class is machine-local-only and never crosses sync, distinct from local_authoritative state that could in principle ride sync.

data_portability_class.description: Portability class for a data class. 'portable' rides sync subject to scope-broadening and allowed-scope rules. 'machine_local' is bound to one device and never syncs. 'excluded' is on the explicit omitted-classes denylist (secret bytes, credentials, trust grants, etc). 'policy_owned' is owned by admin policy and cannot be moved by user sync.

data_class_id.description: Opaque data-class id (for example settings_user_global, machine_local_paths, admin_policy_bundles). Consumers treat this as opaque text.

notes.description: Optional short reviewer-facing note about this data class on this row.

drill_id.description: Stable id for the row's named failure drill, drawn from the envelope's failure_drill_id_vocabulary.

forced_input.description: Forced row mutation applied under --force-drill so the runner can reproduce the named expected_check_id from a clean tree.

expected_check_id.description: Exact check_id the runner must report under --force-drill.

actionable_next_action.description: Short reviewer-readable next action when the drill fires unexpectedly.

failure_drill.description: Named failure drill the validation lane reproduces under --force-drill. Required on every row so omissions stay loud.

offered_resolution_path_classes.description: Resolution paths the row offers. MUST include 'keep_local' on every row so the user always has a safe out, unless the row is in no_conflict / value_equal_no_op state where keep_local is the trivial path and the validation lane allows omission only when conflict_review_class is one of those two.

data_class_portabilities.description: Per-data-class portability classifications the row exposes. Every row publishes at least one entry; the envelope asserts the union covers all four portability classes (portable, machine_local, excluded, policy_owned) across the seed.

last_successful_sync_state_class.description: Coarse last-successful-sync class. 'never_synced' applies to first-registration rows. 'synced_within_freshness_budget' applies to rows whose last accepted epoch is fresh per the resolver budget. 'stale_payload_pending_review' applies to rows whose latest arrival was refused with stale_payload. 'not_applicable' applies to rows that never participate.

rollback_checkpoint_required.description: Whether applying this row's sync resolution requires an ADR-0006 rollback checkpoint. The validation lane forbids merge_preview without a non-null rollback_checkpoint_required pin on protected rows.

approval_ticket_required.description: Whether applying this row's sync resolution requires an ADR-0007 approval ticket.

owner_dri.description: Named owner DRI for the sync-state profile row.

notes.description: Optional short reviewer-facing note about the row.

allOf.description: active rows MUST publish a sync_session_state_class drawn from the active-eligible subset; paused / revoked / forgotten rows MUST publish the corresponding refused or degraded session class.
paused devices MUST publish sync_session_state_class = local_authoritative_degraded or paused and conflict_review_class = device_paused.
rows whose conflict_review_class is scope_broadening_refusal MUST publish scope_broadening_verdict_class = would_widen_trust_refused and offer keep_local in the resolution-path set.
rows whose conflict_review_class is no_conflict or value_equal_no_op MUST publish conflict_resolution_state_class = not_applicable.
